steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Translate equivalent ratios into a proportion: "6 to x is equivalent to y to 7" means $\frac{6}{x} = \frac{y}{7}$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Cross-multiply: $6 \cdot 7 = y \cdot x$, so $42 = xy$. Divide both sides by $y$: $x = \frac{42}{y}$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • A: $x=\frac{6y}{7}$ - this comes from incorrectly writing $\frac{6}{7} = \frac{x}{y}$, treating $x$ as corresponding to 6 and $y$ as corresponding to 7. Incorrect.
  • B: $x=\frac{7y}{6}$ - this would come from incorrectly writing $\frac{6}{7} = \frac{y}{x}$, mixing up the original ratios. Incorrect.
  • C: $x=\frac{y}{42}$ - inverts the derived relationship; it makes $x$ proportional to $y$ instead of inversely proportional. Incorrect.
  • D: $x=\frac{42}{y}$ - matches the result $xy = 42$ derived by cross-multiplication. That matches the correct answer; D is correct.
